title: What Can Wait Until Tomorrow
subtitle: A journal practice for releasing the day's unfinished business before sleep
excerpt: A set of journal prompts for Day 7 of the sleep arc. The writing distinguishes between what needs the night and what can be handed to tomorrow, creating a clear separation between today's work and tonight's rest.
sourceNotes: Theme from Day 7 of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive to Fall Asleep Faster and Wake Energised (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798269649795). Prompt structure original to homemade.education.

content.text: Journal prompts for Day 7. Use them sitting up before bed. The writing creates a clear boundary between what belongs in the evening and what belongs in tomorrow, so the night has a chance to be its own thing.
New to journal prompts as a practice? Read
journal prompts as practice
first.
The practice
Warm-up
What is still running in my head from today?
Write everything that's still unfinished or unresolved. Get it all on the page.
Does any of this actually need tonight?
Go through the list. Mark anything that cannot wait until morning. Be specific about why. The rest can go.
Closer in
What are the three things I most need to do tomorrow?
Write them now so the brain can stop tracking them. Three only. Tomorrow handles the rest.
What would it mean about me if I just stopped now and nothing more got done tonight?
Write the belief that makes stopping feel like failure. Then write whether it's actually true.
What did I do today that was enough?
Name one thing that happened today that counts. It doesn't have to be big.
Where this practice comes from
Built from the Day 7 theme of
SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive
(Rebecca J Page, 2025): unhooking from busyness. Prompt structure original to homemade.education.
